title: Agent Handoff Routing
description: Activity flow for multi-agent handoff: LLM detects transfer tool call, Runner identifies target agent, context/history is filtered, active agent is switched, execution resumes in new agent.
activity_flow: START: Runner.run(starting_agent, input, context, run_config, max_turns)
INIT: Set current_agent = starting_agent; initialize RunContextWrapper; set should_run_agent_start_hooks=True
LOOP: Increment current_turn; check current_turn > max_turns → raise MaxTurnsExceeded
AGENT TURN: Call get_all_tools(current_agent, context_wrapper); get_handoffs(current_agent, context_wrapper)
RENDER HANDOFFS AS TOOLS: Build handoff_map = {handoff.tool_name: Handoff}; each Handoff exposed to LLM as function tool named 'transfer_to_<agent_name>'
LLM INFERENCE: Model generates response with possible function_call outputs
PROCESS RESPONSE: process_model_response() iterates response.output items
CLASSIFY OUTPUT: For each function_tool output, check if output.name in handoff_map
BRANCH: If output.name matches a handoff → create ToolRunHandoff(handoff, tool_call); else treat as regular FunctionTool
ProcessedResponse: Collects run_handoffs: list[ToolRunHandoff], functions, computer_actions, etc.
DECISION: Does ProcessedResponse.handoffs have entries?
NO → execute normal tool calls, check for final output, loop back
YES → enter execute_handoffs() path
MULTIPLE HANDOFFS CHECK: If len(run_handoffs) > 1 → append 'Multiple handoffs detected, ignoring this one.' ToolCallOutputItem for all but first; record span error
SELECT FIRST HANDOFF: actual_handoff = run_handoffs[0]
INVOKE HANDOFF: new_agent = await handoff.on_invoke_handoff(context_wrapper, tool_call.arguments)
on_invoke_handoff internally: if input_type set → validate JSON via TypeAdapter, call on_handoff(ctx, validated_input); else call on_handoff(ctx) if provided
on_invoke_handoff always returns the captured agent instance
CREATE HandoffOutputItem: raw_item = tool_call_output_item(tool_call, '{"assistant": new_agent.name}'); append to new_step_items
FIRE HOOKS (parallel): hooks.on_handoff(context, from_agent, to_agent) + agent.hooks.on_handoff(context, agent=new_agent, source=from_agent)
RESOLVE INPUT FILTER: input_filter = handoff.input_filter OR run_config.handoff_input_filter (handoff-level takes precedence)
RESOLVE NEST HISTORY: should_nest_history = handoff.nest_handoff_history if set, else run_config.nest_handoff_history
SERVER-MANAGED CHECK: If server-managed conversation (conversation_id/previous_response_id) and input_filter set → raise UserError; if nest_history set → warn and disable
DECISION: input_filter or should_nest_history?
IF input_filter set:
Build HandoffInputData(input_history, pre_handoff_items, new_items, run_context)
filtered = await input_filter(handoff_input_data)
Extract: original_input=filtered.input_history, pre_step_items=filtered.pre_handoff_items, new_step_items=filtered.new_items
If filtered.input_items set: session_step_items=filtered.new_items, new_step_items=filtered.input_items (split session vs model input)
ELIF should_nest_history:
Build HandoffInputData, call nest_handoff_history(data, run_config.handoff_history_mapper)
Collapses prior transcript into <CONVERSATION HISTORY> summary block
session_step_items = full new_items; new_step_items = filtered input_items if available
ELSE: No filtering; pass full history to new agent
RETURN SingleStepResult(next_step=NextStepHandoff(new_agent), original_input, model_response, new_step_items, session_step_items)
RUNNER RECEIVES NextStepHandoff:
current_agent = turn_result.next_step.new_agent
run_state._current_agent = current_agent
Finish current tracing span (reset_current=True); current_span = None
should_run_agent_start_hooks = True
(Streaming only): emit AgentUpdatedStreamEvent(new_agent=current_agent) to event queue
run_state._current_step = NextStepRunAgain()
continue → back to LOOP top
NEW AGENT LOOP: New agent runs with filtered/nested history; process continues until FinalOutput or MaxTurns
END: Return RunResult with last_agent = final current_agent

key_data_structures.Handoff: tool_name: str, tool_description: str, input_json_schema: dict, on_invoke_handoff: Callable[[RunContextWrapper, str], Awaitable[Agent]], agent_name: str, input_filter: HandoffInputFilter|None, nest_handoff_history: bool|None, strict_json_schema: bool=True, is_enabled: bool|Callable
key_data_structures.HandoffInputData: input_history: str|tuple[TResponseInputItem,...], pre_handoff_items: tuple[RunItem,...], new_items: tuple[RunItem,...], run_context: RunContextWrapper|None, input_items: tuple[RunItem,...]|None
key_data_structures.ProcessedResponse: new_items, handoffs: list[ToolRunHandoff], functions, computer_actions, tools_used, interruptions
key_data_structures.SingleStepResult: original_input, model_response, pre_step_items, new_step_items, next_step: NextStepHandoff|NextStepFinalOutput|NextStepRunAgain|NextStepInterruption, session_step_items: list|None

key_methods: Runner.run(starting_agent, input, context, run_config, max_turns) -> RunResult
process_model_response(agent, all_tools, response, output_schema, handoffs) -> ProcessedResponse
execute_handoffs(public_agent, original_input, pre_step_items, new_step_items, run_handoffs, hooks, context_wrapper, run_config) -> SingleStepResult
_invoke_handoff(ctx, input_json) [internal closure]: validates input, calls on_handoff callback, returns captured agent
handoff(agent, tool_name_override, tool_description_override, on_handoff, input_type, input_filter, nest_handoff_history, is_enabled) -> Handoff
decision_branches: Multiple handoffs in one turn? → Only first processed; rest get 'Multiple handoffs detected' output + span error
input_type set? → Validate JSON payload via TypeAdapter; call on_handoff(ctx, validated_input)
on_handoff without input_type? → Call on_handoff(ctx) for side effects only
input_filter set (handoff or run_config)? → Filter HandoffInputData before passing to new agent
nest_handoff_history enabled? → Collapse prior transcript into summary block
input_items in filtered result? → Split: session sees full new_items; model sees filtered input_items
Server-managed conversation + nest_history? → Warning, history nesting disabled
is_enabled=False (bool or callable returns false)? → Handoff hidden from LLM tool list at runtime
lifecycle_hooks: Both fire in parallel (asyncio.gather) after new_agent is resolved
tracing: handoff_span(from_agent=public_agent.name) wraps execute_handoffs; span_data.to_agent = new_agent.name set after resolution
Multiple handoffs attach SpanError(message='Multiple handoffs requested', data={'requested_agents': [...]})
Current agent_span finished and reset to None on handoff; new span created for new agent in next loop iteration
business_rules: Handoffs stay within a single run — they do NOT start a new Runner.run() call
Input guardrails apply only to the first agent; output guardrails only to the agent producing final output
New agent sees full conversation history by default unless input_filter or nest_handoff_history is set
handoff() helper always transfers to the specific agent captured — use on_handoff for side effects, not dynamic routing
For dynamic destination selection at invocation time, use a custom Handoff subclass with custom on_invoke_handoff
Disabled handoffs (is_enabled=False) are hidden from LLM tool list at runtime
